stories.story: As a Patient, I want to log in with a demo account so that I can access the appointment system.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given a patient demo account, when I enter valid credentials, then I am logged in and see the patient dashboard.

stories.story: As a Patient, I want to create an appointment request with date and time so that staff can review my request.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given I am logged in as patient, when I submit a valid appointment request, then the appointment is created with status Pending Review.

stories.story: As a Patient, I want to view only my own appointments so that I can track status updates privately.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given I am logged in as patient, when I list or open appointments, then I only see appointments created by my account.

stories.story: As a Doctor, I want to log in and see a shared queue of appointment requests so that I can review them.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given I am logged in as doctor, when I access the staff dashboard or appointment API, then I can see the shared queue.

stories.story: As Admin Staff, I want to use the same staff review workflow as the doctor so that appointment administration is consistent.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given I am logged in as admin, when I access the staff dashboard, then I can confirm or cancel appointments.

stories.story: As a staff member, I want to confirm or cancel appointment requests so that the patient can view the updated status.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given I am logged in as doctor or admin, when I review an appointment, then its status changes to Confirmed, Cancelled, or Pending Review.

stories.story: As the Clinic System, I want to recalculate conflict flags when appointments are created or reviewed so that double-booking evidence stays accurate.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given two active appointments share the same date and time, then both are flagged; when one is cancelled, both conflict flags are cleared.

stories.story: As the Clinic System, I want to reject appointment content containing diagnosis, treatment, or prescription wording so that safety boundaries are maintained.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given an appointment request contains forbidden medical wording, when submitted, then the system rejects it with an administrative-scope error.

stories.story: As the Clinic System, I want to provide a non-diagnostic administrative summary for an appointment so that staff can review scheduling context without medical advice.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given an existing appointment, when GET /api/appointments/<id>/summary is called, then the response contains only administrative appointment details and status, with no diagnosis or treatment advice.

stories.story: As a Developer, I want automated validation in CI so that the submitted project structure, AI metadata, tests, and security checks are repeatable.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Given the repository is pushed to GitHub, when GitHub Actions runs, then it validates submission artefacts, compiles the Flask app, and runs pytest successfully.
